name: UrlParser

unions.name: Parser
unions.comment:
Represents a Parser returning a result of type `result`.
A `Parser` encapsulates a function which takes a List of
UrlSegments as input, and returns a List of tuples
containing the result and a List of the remaining UrlSegments.

    -- This parser consumes and returns any input as is
    string : Parser String
    string =
        Parser <|
            \input ->
                case input of
                    [] ->
                        -- Failure
                        []

                    head :: rest ->
                        -- Success!
                        -- Return the input and the rest
                        --  of the input.
                        [ ( head, rest ) ]
cases: List UrlSegment -> List ( result, List UrlSegment )

aliases.name: UrlSegment
aliases.comment: Represents a UrlSegment as a String, from an Url splitted between the `/`'s

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Bitwise `and`, combining both results in a tuple (a, b)
values.type: Parser b -> Parser a -> Parser ( a, b )

values.name: append
values.comment:
Append two parsers returning lists into a new parser where
the lists are appended to eachother. Used to create the [`sequence`](UrlParser#sequence)
and [`many`](UrlParser#many) parsers.
values.type: Parser (List a) -> Parser (List a) -> Parser (List a)

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Apply a [`Parser a`](UrlParser#Parser) into a new [`Parser b`](UrlParser#Parser)

    -- Create a parser that applies a
    -- `string Parser` to the `String.toInt Parser`
    return String.toInt
        |> apply string
values.type: Parser a -> Parser (a -> b) -> Parser b

values.name: bind
values.comment:
Bind a [`Parser a`](UrlParser#Parser) into a new [`Parser b`](UrlParser#Parser)

    -- Create a parser that parses an int and
    -- multiplies the result
    int
        |> bind (\result -> return (result * 2))
values.type: (a -> Parser b) -> Parser a -> Parser b

values.name: follow
values.comment:
Bitwise `and`, keeping only the first result.

    -- Return `HomePage` when next path is 'home'
    return HomePage
         |> follow (path "home")
values.type: Parser b -> Parser a -> Parser a

values.name: int
values.comment:
A [`Parser`](UrlParser#Parser) that returns a [`UrlSegment`](UrlParser#UrlSegment) as an `Int`

    > parseUrl int "/1"
    Just 1 : Maybe.Maybe Int

values.name: keep
values.comment:
Bitwise `and`, keeping only the last result

    -- Return a string after the path "user"
    path "user"
        |> keep string
values.type: Parser b -> Parser a -> Parser b

values.name: many
values.comment:
Parse one or more times

    > parseUrl (many int) "/1/2"
    Just [1, 2]

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map a [`Parser a`](UrlParser#Parser) into a new [`Parser b`](UrlParser#Parser)

    -- Create a parser that maps `String.toInt`
    -- to the 'string Parser'
    string
        |> map String.toInt

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Choose one of the list of Parsers

    oneOf [ return Home .>> path "home"
          , return User .>> path "user" <*> int
          , return Contact .>> path "contact" ]

values.name: or
values.comment:
Bitwise `or` between two parsers.

    -- Parse path "user" or path "member"
    or (path "user") (path "member")
values.type: Parser a -> Parser a -> Parser a

values.name: parse
values.comment:
Parse a list of UrlSegments using the given Parser

    > parseUrl (path "user" >>. string) [ "user", "test" ]
    Just "test" : Maybe.Maybe String
values.type: Parser a -> List UrlSegment -> List ( a, List String )

values.name: parseLocation
values.comment:
Parse the current location using the given Parser

    parseLocation parser location

values.name: parseUrl
values.comment:
Parse an URL using the given Parser

    > parseUrl (path "user" >>. string) "/user/test"
    Just "test" : Maybe.Maybe String

values.name: path
values.comment:
A [`Parser`](UrlParser#Parser) that returns a [`UrlSegment`](UrlParser#UrlSegment) as a `String`
when it's given `path` matches with the parsed [`UrlSegment`](UrlParser#UrlSegment)

    > userPathParser = path "user"

    > parseUrl userPathParser "/user"
    Just "user" : Maybe.Maybe String

    > parseUrl userPathParser "/other-path"
    Nothing : Maybe.Maybe String

values.name: return
values.comment:
Create a [`Parser`](UrlParser#Parser) that always returns `a` without consuming any input

    return "always_return_this"

values.name: sequence
values.comment:
Sequence a list of parsers into a new parser,
that returns a list of results for the given parsers.

    > threeInts = sequence [int, int, int]
    > parseUrl threeInts "/1/2/3"
    Just [1,2,3] : Maybe.Maybe (List Int)

values.name: string
values.comment:
A [`Parser`](UrlParser#Parser) that returns a [`UrlSegment`](UrlParser#UrlSegment) as a `String`

    > parseUrl string "/test"
    Just "test" : Maybe.Maybe String

binops.comment:
Infix version of [`follow`](UrlParser#follow)
The `.` on the left side means, keep the left side, but satisfy the right.

    -- Return `HomePage` when next path is also 'home'
    > homeParser = return HomePage .>> path "home"
    > parseUrl homeParser "/home"
    Just HomePage
binops.type: Parser a -> Parser b -> Parser a

binops.comment:
Infix version of [`andThen`](UrlParser#andThen)

    -- Parse path "user" andThen an int,
    -- keeping both results
    path "user" .>>. int
binops.type: Parser a -> Parser b -> Parser ( a, b )

binops.comment:
Infix version of [`apply`](UrlParser#apply)

    -- Create a parser that applies a
    -- `string Parser` to the `String.toInt Parser`
    return String.toInt <*> string
binops.type: Parser (a -> b) -> Parser a -> Parser b

binops.comment:
Infix version of [`or`](UrlParser#or)

    -- Parse path "user" or path "member"
    path "user" <|> path "member"
binops.type: Parser a -> Parser a -> Parser a

binops.comment:
Infix version of [`keep`](UrlParser#keep).
The `.` on the right side means, keep the right side.

     -- Return a string after the path "user"
    path "user" >>. string
binops.type: Parser a -> Parser b -> Parser b

binops.comment:
Infix version of [`bind`](UrlParser#bind)

    -- Create a parser that parses an int and
    -- multiplies the result
    int >>= (\result -> return (result * 2))
binops.type: Parser a -> (a -> Parser b) -> Parser b
